glioma tumor: On brain MRI, a glioma often appears as an infiltrative mass with irregular margins and surrounding edema.
Glioma in MRI shows heterogeneous signal intensity and possible central necrosis.
meningioma tumor: A meningioma on MRI is typically a well-circumscribed extra-axial mass with broad dural attachment.
Meningioma shows homogeneous enhancement and a dural tail sign on contrast MRI.
no tumor: A normal brain MRI shows symmetric hemispheres with no abnormal mass or signal.
No-tumor MRI presents intact gray-white differentiation and normal ventricles.
pituitary tumor: A pituitary tumor on MRI is centered in the sella turcica, sometimes with suprasellar extension.
Pituitary adenoma shows a focal sellar mass that may displace the optic chiasm.
